ddls:
CREATE TABLE "respondents" (
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each respondent */,
  "age" TEXT /* Age of the respondent (e.g., 65 and older, 50 to 64) */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the respondent (e.g., Graduate or Professional Degree, Some college or trade school) */,
  "file_race" TEXT /* File race of the respondent (e.g., White, Hispanic) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the respondent (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "race_eth" TEXT /* Race and ethnicity of the respondent (e.g., White, Hispanic) */,
  "respondent_email" TEXT /* Email address of the respondent for communication */,
  "respondent_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the respondent for communication */,
  "respondent_address" TEXT /* Address of the respondent for demographic analysis */,
  "respondent_state" TEXT /* State of residence for demographic analysis */,
  "respondent_city" TEXT /* City of residence for demographic analysis */,
  "respondent_zip" TEXT /* Zip code of the respondent for demographic analysis */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the respondent for more precise age calculations */,
  "date_of_registration" TEXT /* Date the respondent was registered in the system */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data (e.g., online survey, phone call) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("respondent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_gender FOREIGN KEY ("gender") REFERENCES genders ("gender_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_race_eth FOREIGN KEY ("race_eth") REFERENCES ethnicities ("ethnicity_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_file_race FOREIGN KEY ("file_race") REFERENCES races ("race_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_age FOREIGN KEY ("age") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_education FOREIGN KEY ("education") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_level_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "questions" (
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each question */,
  "question_text" TEXT /* Text of the question */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response scale used (e.g., approve/disapprove, yes/no) */,
  "question_category" TEXT /* Category of the question (e.g., political, social) */,
  "question_subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the question (e.g., presidential performance, party preference) */,
  "question_version" TEXT /* Version of the question to track changes over time */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date the question was created */,
  "date_modified" TEXT /* Date the question was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("question_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "question_id" INTEGER /* ID of the question */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response given by the respondent */,
  "date_responded" TEXT /* Date the response was given */,
  "response_version" TEXT /* Version of the response to track changes over time */,
  "response_confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the response (e.g., 0.8 for high confidence) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_question_id FOREIGN KEY ("question_id") REFERENCES questions ("question_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group (e.g., 65 and older, 50 to 64) */,
  "age_group_description" TEXT /* Description of the age group for additional context */,
  "age_group_range" TEXT /* Range of ages in the group (e.g., 65-74, 75-84) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level (e.g., Graduate or Professional Degree, Some college or trade school) */,
  "education_level_description" TEXT /* Description of the education level for additional context */,
  "education_level_category" TEXT /* Category of the education level (e.g., higher education, secondary education) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race (e.g., White, Hispanic) */,
  "race_description" TEXT /* Description of the race for additional context */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "gender_description" TEXT /* Description of the gender for additional context */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ethnicities" (
  "ethnicity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ethnicity */,
  "ethnicity_name" TEXT /* Name of the ethnicity (e.g., White, Hispanic) */,
  "ethnicity_description" TEXT /* Description of the ethnicity for additional context */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ethnicity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weights" (
  "weight_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weight */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "weight_type" TEXT /* Type of weight (e.g., final weight, turnout score) */,
  "weight_value" REAL /* Value of the weight */,
  "date_weighted" TEXT /* Date the weight was calculated */,
  "weight_source" TEXT /* Source of the weight (e.g., survey, census) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weight_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weights_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "turnout" (
  "turnout_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each turnout record */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "turnout_class" TEXT /* Class of turnout (e.g., 50 to 85 percent) */,
  "phone_type" TEXT /* Type of phone used (e.g., Cell) */,
  "turnout_scale" TEXT /* Scale used to measure turnout (e.g., 1, 0.7778) */,
  "turnout_score" REAL /* Score of the turnout */,
  "date_turnout" TEXT /* Date the turnout information was recorded */,
  "turnout_source" TEXT /* Source of the turnout information (e.g., historical data, survey) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("turnout_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_turnout_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)
